title: agent-staff-engineer ops.config
description: Contract between the portable agent bundle and any target project. Every skill, rule, template, and script reads this file via scripts/lib/schema.mjs. Projects never hand-write this file; bootstrap-ops-config produces it from live project state plus the interview answers.

$schemaVersion.description: Schema version this config targets. Installer compares against the bundled schema version and triggers --update if they diverge.

name.description: Short project name. Used in CLAUDE.md, PR bodies, report headers.

org.description: Optional GitHub org or user owning the primary repo. Derived from repo field if absent.

repo.description: Primary repo in owner/name form (GitHub-style ASCII identifiers only).

default_branch.description: Default branch to open PRs against.

push_allowed.description: GitHub logins or identifiers permitted to push to the default branch. The agent never pushes to this branch itself.

reviewers_default.description: GitHub logins requested as reviewers on every PR the agent opens. May include Copilot.

trackers.description: Issue / PR / project trackers the agent operates against. `dev` hosts day-to-day issues and code review; `release` hosts release umbrellas; `observed` is a read-only array for cross-tracker lookups (e.g. during a migration or for shared libs on a different tracker). Each entry is a tracker target discriminated by the required `kind` field (github, jira, linear, gitlab).

observed.description: Read-only tracker targets the agent may query but never mutates. Typical uses: cross-repo regression lookups, dual-reading during a tracker migration.

migration.description: Optional sidecar recording an in-flight tracker migration. Written by adapt-system's `tracker:migrate:<from>:<to>` op and consulted by skills that emit cross-link comments during the dual-read window. Absent when no migration is in progress.

from.description: Outgoing tracker kind. Appended to trackers.observed[] with depth:read-only when the migration starts.

to.description: Incoming tracker kind. Becomes trackers.dev (and optionally trackers.release) on the migration's announce stage.

started_at.description: ISO 8601 datetime when the migration was announced (adapt-system stage 1).

cutover_at.description: ISO 8601 datetime when the migration cut over (adapt-system stage 3). null while dual-read is in effect.

notes.description: Free-form note captured from the user's intent text at announce time.

workspace.description: Optional multi-repo workspace binding. When present, each member declares its own tracker(s); a branch created under that member's path routes through its tracker instead of the project-level one. Absent for single-repo projects.

path.description: Project-relative path of this workspace member. Use '.' for the primary repo.

name.description: Short identifier for the member, used in logs and status updates.

labels.description: Prefixed label scopes. Skills reason about labels by scope prefix, not by hardcoded names.

type.description: Scope type/*. Example values: feature, bug, task, refactor, docs, chore.

area.description: Scope area/*. Project-specific. Example defaults: frontend, backend, data, security, performance, compliance, ux, devx, testing, docs.

priority.description: Scope priority/*. Example values: p0-blocker, p1-high, p2-medium, p3-low.

intent.description: Scope intent/*. One release umbrella is created per intent value. Example: initial, wave-1, wave-2, post-launch.

size.description: Scope size/*. Example values: xs, s, m, l, xl.

automation.description: Scope automation/*. Labels the agent itself applies. Example: auto-regression, auto-release-tracked.

state_modifiers.description: Labels stacked on top of status to express blocked, deferred, cancelled, and similar modifiers.

phase_term.description: The word used in label scope intent/* and in release umbrella titles. Default: wave.

branch_patterns.description: Branch name templates per type. Placeholders: {issue}, {slug}.

signed.description: Whether commits are GPG or SSH signed. Agent respects this but does not configure keys.

body_template.description: Path to the PR body template. May be bundle-relative (agents/agent-staff-engineer/templates/pr.md) or project-relative.

link_issue_with.description: Template for the issue link line. Placeholder: {issue}.

request_reviewers.description: Reviewers the agent requests on every PR. Usually references principals.reviewers_default plus Copilot if available.

tests_required.description: Test categories that must pass locally before the PR opens.

e2e_required_on.description: Area labels that trigger the e2e test gate. Empty means e2e is never required.

self_review_required.description: If true, dev-loop files a self-review report under paths.reports before opening the PR.

link_release_umbrella.description: If true, the agent updates the linked release umbrella when a PR opens or closes.

update_plan_oneliner.description: If true, plan-keeper updates the one-liner checkbox when the gate crosses.

umbrella_title.description: Title template for Release umbrella issues. Placeholder: {intent_label_pretty}.

code_review.description: Self-review / code-review provider used by dev-loop before opening a PR. Defaults to the external ctxr-dev/skill-code-review skill.

provider.description: ctxr-skill-code-review invokes the external multi-specialist skill. internal-template uses the bundle's code-review-report.md as a fallback. none skips the self-review step entirely (only allowed if workflow.pr.self_review_required is false).

provider_url.description: Canonical source URL for the selected provider (informational).

invocation.description: Command or slash-skill the dev-loop issues to run the review. For ctxr-skill-code-review this is usually "/skill-code-review".

mode.description: Provider-specific mode hint. For ctxr-skill-code-review: diff (default), full, thorough, fast.

report_dir.description: Where the review artefact lands. Must be inside paths.dev_working_dir. Defaults to paths.reports.

block_on_verdict.description: Verdict values that block the PR from opening. For ctxr-skill-code-review the verdict space is GO / CONDITIONAL / NO-GO.

install_hint.description: One-line hint shown if the provider is configured but not installed in the target project.

external_review.description: Post-push external-reviewer loop (e.g. Copilot on GitHub). Drives the pr-iteration skill: request review, poll for CI and review, fetch unresolved threads, triage, resolve, iterate until all three exit conditions hold (local review GO, zero unresolved, CI SUCCESS on HEAD). Disable by setting enabled:false.

enabled.description: When false, dev-loop skips the pr-iteration hand-off and halts at 'In review'. Useful for projects that don't use an external reviewer.

provider.description: auto picks the impl from trackers.dev.kind via scripts/lib/trackers/dispatcher.mjs (pickReviewProvider). github forces the GitHub impl regardless of tracker kind (used when the tracker is elsewhere but code review runs on GitHub). none is equivalent to enabled:false.

bots.description: Per-kind map of reviewer bot LOGINS that requestReview registers. For GitHub each value is the bot's login (e.g. copilot-pull-request-reviewer), portable across repos. The pr-iteration orchestration layer resolves login -> GraphQL node ID before invoking the GitHub provider (which itself requires ctx.botIds as node IDs); see rules/pr-iteration.md for the resolution recipe. Config stores logins (not node IDs) because node IDs vary across installations and would pin the config to a specific GitHub deployment.

poll_interval_seconds.description: Seconds between poll-for-review probes. 30 is the runbook default; under 15 burns rate limit, over 300 crosses the harness cache-miss boundary.

poll_timeout_seconds.description: Upper bound on the poll loop before surfacing a timeout to the human. 1200 = 20 minutes, enough for a typical Copilot round; longer for CI suites that run >20 min.

auto_resolve_stale_after_commits.description: When the same path:line thread has been re-emitted by the reviewer on a superseded SHA (commonly 30-50% of Copilot threads after round 1 per the runbook), auto-resolve it after this many commits have landed without changing the underlying code. Set 0 to disable and require manual triage for every thread.

paths.description: Working paths inside the target project. The agent reads and writes only inside these. It never writes under daily/ or knowledge/.

agent_bundle_dir.description: Where the agent-staff-engineer bundle lives inside the target project. The bundle remains its own git repo at this path; updates happen via 'git pull' inside the folder. All canonical skills, rules, memory seeds, templates, schemas, and scripts stay here and never copy out.

wrappers.description: Where install.mjs writes thin wrapper files that point at canonical content inside agent_bundle_dir. These are the only files the installer writes outside the bundle folder. Every wrapper contains a marker line; content above the marker is regenerated on every 'install.mjs --update' run, content below is preserved byte-for-byte so user overrides survive updates.

skills_dir.description: Directory where skill wrappers are written. One <name>/SKILL.md per canonical skill.

rules_dir.description: Directory where rule wrappers are written. One <name>.md per canonical rule.

marker.description: Exact string that delimits the auto-regenerated section from user overrides. Every wrapper contains this marker on its own line. Above the marker is refreshed on every update. Below the marker is preserved byte-for-byte.
marker.default: <!-- ============ PROJECT OVERRIDES BELOW (preserved across agent updates) ============ -->

header_notice.description: Short notice rendered at the top of every wrapper explaining the model in one sentence.
header_notice.default: Wrapper file. Canonical content lives in the agent bundle and updates via 'git pull' inside the bundle. Edit below the overrides marker only; everything above is regenerated.

preserve_user_below_marker.description: If true (default), install.mjs never touches bytes below the marker on update. If false, install.mjs rewrites the entire wrapper on every update (not recommended; loses user customisation).

plans_root.description: Root of the plan folder tree.

plan_states.description: State folders under plans_root. Only the last entry (typically done) is nested by date.

done_nested.description: If true, the done/ state folder is nested under {yyyy}/{mm}/{dd}/.

done_pattern.description: Pattern relative to plans_root when a plan moves to done. Placeholders: {yyyy}, {mm}, {dd}, {slug}.

dev_working_dir.description: Working directory for generated artefacts. Splits into shared/ (committed), local/ (gitignored), and cache/ (gitignored) subtrees via dev_working_{shared,local,cache}_subdir. Never equal to a daily/ or knowledge/ path.

gitignore_dev_working_dir.description: If true, installer ensures per-user subfolders under dev_working_dir (local/, cache/) are listed in .gitignore. The shared/ subtree is deliberately NOT ignored so team conventions, reports, and runbooks commit with the project.

dev_working_shared_subdir.description: Sub-path (relative to dev_working_dir) that holds committed, team-shared content (configs, reports, runbooks). Must be a single path segment or relative nested path; no leading slash, no '..'.

dev_working_local_subdir.description: Sub-path (relative to dev_working_dir) that holds per-user artefacts. Always gitignored. Must be a single path segment or relative nested path; no leading slash, no '..'.

dev_working_cache_subdir.description: Sub-path (relative to dev_working_dir) that holds regenerable caches. Always gitignored. Must be a single path segment or relative nested path; no leading slash, no '..'.

templates.description: Where templates live. Usually a bundle-relative path so updates to the bundle reach existing installs.

reports.description: Where self-review and other generated reports land. Defaults into the shared subtree so the team sees review provenance; configurable to local/ when a project prefers per-user reports.

runbooks.description: Where skill-authored runbooks land. Defaults into the shared subtree (team-visible).

wiki.description: Routing of docs under .development/** through an LLM wiki skill. Every topical subfolder under shared/, local/, and cache/ is managed as an in-place wiki by the provider skill. The agent invokes the skill (see rules/llm-wiki.md) for all reads and writes so the wiki format stays owned by the skill, not duplicated here.

provider.description: npm package name of the wiki skill. Currently only @ctxr/skill-llm-wiki is supported.

mode.description: Layout mode the skill uses. Only 'in-place' is supported today: each topic folder IS its wiki, no sibling .wiki/ folder.

roots.description: Absolute-relative-to-project paths for the three wiki scopes. Skills read these so they never hard-code '.development/shared' literals.

shared.description: Committed wiki root; every subfolder is a team-shared topic wiki.

local.description: Per-user wiki root (gitignored); every subfolder is a per-user topic wiki.

cache.description: Regenerable wiki root (gitignored); every subfolder is a regenerable topic wiki.

shared_topics.description: Topic wikis the installer pre-seeds under shared/ on first apply. Extra topics appear lazily when the agent first writes into them.

required.description: When true, installer refuses to apply if the provider skill is not installed. Set false only when the project manages .development/ manually.

stack.description: Tags describing the project's tech. Controls which memory seeds install and which test commands dev-loop runs.

language.description: Primary programming languages used in the project.

testing.description: Testing frameworks used by the project.

area_keywords.description: Maps each area/* label value to a list of keywords. Used by regression-handler lookups and by rollout-to-issues conversion.

compliance.description: Compliance context declared during the bootstrap interview. Adapt-system may expand this later. Empty means none.

data_classes.description: Sensitive data classes handled by the project. Influences proposed rules and labels.

depthValue.description: Observation depth for a tracker target. `labeled:<label>` is the only parameterized form; it narrows operation to items carrying the named label.

statusValues.description: Mapping of the agent's status vocabulary to the tracker's native status names (e.g. Project v2 option names on GitHub, workflow state names on Jira/Linear, scoped label values on GitLab).

trackerTarget.description: Discriminated union over the four supported tracker kinds. The required `kind` field picks the per-kind shape.

githubTracker.description: GitHub tracker target. One or more Projects v2 boards backing issues/PRs in a repo.

owner.description: Org or user that owns the repo and the Projects v2 boards.

repo.description: Repo name (no owner prefix). Required for dev trackers; optional for release trackers that span multiple repos in the same org.

auth_login.description: The gh login under which the agent operates. Informational; skills read live gh state for auth.

projects.description: GitHub Projects v2 boards this tracker owns. Zero entries disables project-field operations on this tracker.

githubProjectV2.description: One GitHub Projects v2 board bound to a githubTracker.

owner.description: Org or user that owns the Project v2 board (usually the same as the parent tracker's owner, but Projects v2 can span orgs).

fields.description: Custom project fields the agent expects or reconciles.

label_scope.description: If set, agent only operates on items carrying this label.

jiraTracker.description: Atlassian Jira Cloud tracker target. Skills authenticate via the JIRA_API_TOKEN env var (or delegate to jira-cli if present).

site.description: Jira Cloud site hostname, e.g. acme.atlassian.net.

labels_field.description: Jira custom field holding the agent's label taxonomy. Default is the built-in 'labels' field.

linearTracker.description: Linear tracker target. Skills authenticate via the LINEAR_API_KEY env var.

workspace.description: Linear workspace URL key (the part of linear.app/<workspace>).

team.description: Linear team key (usually 2-5 uppercase letters shown in issue IDs like ENG-123).

gitlabTracker.description: GitLab tracker target (gitlab.com or self-hosted). Skills authenticate via the GITLAB_TOKEN env var (or delegate to glab if present).

host.description: GitLab host, e.g. gitlab.com or gitlab.acme.internal.

project_path.description: Full GitLab project path including groups and subgroups, e.g. acme/platform/billing.
